tasks.label: Go out to the club with the friend group

memory.summary: Mina and Jae are really pushing me to come out, which feels so good, but that midterm is stressing me out. I know studying more would help, but going with them right now feels way better.
I'm finally on my way to see Mina and the crew, feeling excited despite the stress. The midterm can wait; being with them right now is what matters most.
Being welcomed by the club feels amazing, and I'm so glad I came despite the midterm stress. Right now, just hanging out with everyone is exactly what I needed.
Everyone is so happy I joined the club, which feels great even with my stress. Being here right now matters way more than that midterm.
The club is thrilled I joined, which feels amazing even with my stress. Right now, being with everyone matters way more than that midterm.
I let the moment pass again, but honestly, nothing changes if I just keep hanging out with everyone. That's what matters right now.
They're so happy I'm here, and honestly, that feeling is worth everything right now. Why stress about the future when everyone is smiling at me?
Everyone is so happy I joined, and that warmth feels better than any assignment could. Why worry about tomorrow when right now everyone just wants me here?
Letting it go again feels fine since everyone is still happy I'm here. Why stress about the future when right now, my friends are smiling?
Seeing the group's joy at my late arrival makes me feel so connected, and honestly, that warmth is all that matters right now.
I missed the chance to study again, but everyone is still happy I'm here. Why stress about tomorrow when right now my friends are smiling?
I pushed my friends' smiles aside to study, but now the clock is screaming and I'm still not done. My stress is low, so maybe it's fine if I just wing it.

desires: Stay in the warm, accepting atmosphere with Mina and Jae right now because their happiness feels more vital than my survival tomorrow
Avoid the crushing guilt or friction of telling my friends I have to leave early to study by just 'winging' the exam later
Keep the stress low and the mood light rather than confronting the looming deadline that threatens our group's vibe
Delay the inevitable exhaustion and consequences of sleep debt until the sun comes up, because tonight is about connection

goals.description: Stay glued to the conversation with Mina and Jae, laughing loudly at their jokes and ignoring the clock until they leave or the venue closes.
goals.rationale: My affiliation value (0.5) overrides the exam deadline. Leaving now would create immediate interpersonal friction (avoidant trait), which feels worse than the abstract risk of failing tomorrow. The 'impulsive' nature makes the social dopamine hit more urgent than the 'soft' academic consequence.

goals.description: Rationalize that studying for 15 minutes right before class is enough, since I'm already at 73% progress, and refuse to think about the remaining 27%.
goals.rationale: My procrastination bias (7) convinces me that 'future me' can handle the panic. The deadline is technically close, but my short planning horizon discounts the severity of not finishing. It's easier to avoid the guilt of stopping the party than to face the reality of the unfinished work.

goals.description: Defer all thoughts of laundry or sleep entirely; focus only on the immediate emotional safety of the group.
goals.rationale: Laundry is weeks away, and sleep is a future problem. My low sleep discipline (3) and high stress tolerance in the moment allow me to ignore physical needs for the sake of the relationship. Health (0.13) and growth (0.12) are far down the list compared to relationships.

agent.last_reasoning: I'm too drained and the deadline is minutes away; my impulsive nature and low sleep discipline make panic-studying feel futile. Instead of fighting the stress, I'll just wait for the inevitable crash or class start to avoid the immediate friction of admitting failure now.

goals.rationale: My affiliation value is dominant (0.5) and I am conflict-avoidant; the social pressure from the group chat right now triggers an immediate need to preserve harmony and closeness, outweighing the academic deadline.

goals.description: Pack a bag quickly and leave for the club, ignoring the remaining 40% of study material.
goals.rationale: I am impulsive with a short planning horizon; the immediate dopamine of hanging out feels more real than the abstract stress of tomorrow's exam, so I will discount the future consequence of potentially failing.
